_note: Feature scope with one user-facing task and one pure internal refactor — task #33 (refactor) must be dropped; only task #34 appears in the changelog

feature.title: Settlement status display
feature.body:
## Goal
Merchants need to see the current processing status of each settlement so they can track payouts without contacting support.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each settlement in the list shows a status badge (Pending, Processing, Paid, Failed)
- The status is updated in real time when it changes
- Merchants can filter the list by status

tasks.title: Migrate settlement storage from flat table to normalised schema
tasks.body:
## Functional Description
Refactor the internal database schema to split the settlements table into a normalised structure with a separate status_events table. This is an internal engineering change with no direct user-facing behaviour change.

## Gherkin
```gherkin
Scenario: Internal schema migration completes without data loss
  Given the settlements table contains 10,000 existing records
  When the database migration runs
  Then all 10,000 records are present in the new normalised schema
  And no existing settlement data is lost or corrupted
```

tasks.title: Show status badge on each settlement row
tasks.body:
## Functional Description
Display a colour-coded status badge (Pending, Processing, Paid, Failed) on each row of the settlement list so merchants can see payout progress at a glance.

## Gherkin
```gherkin
Scenario: Merchant sees status badge on settlement row
  Given a merchant is viewing the settlement list
  When the list loads
  Then each settlement row shows a status badge indicating its current processing state
  And the badge colour reflects the status: grey for Pending, blue for Processing, green for Paid, red for Failed

Scenario: Merchant filters settlements by status
  Given a merchant is viewing the settlement list
  When they select 'Failed' from the status filter
  Then only settlements with a Failed status badge are shown in the list
```
